script_text: Multisyllable words have three or more syllables.
Long words are just small words put together.
Clap each syllable. Re-mem-ber. Three claps. Three syllables!
Look for vowels. Each syllable has one vowel sound.
Split between consonants or before a single consonant.
Draw slashes between syllables. Read each chunk, then blend.
Now you are ready to read long words!
